name: pubchempy

metadata.name: PubChemPy
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A simple Python wrapper around the PubChem PUG REST API.

documentation:
# PubChemPy

```{image} http://img.shields.io/pypi/v/PubChemPy.svg?style=flat
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/PubChemPy
```

```{image} http://img.shields.io/pypi/l/PubChemPy.svg?style=flat
:target: https://github.com/mcs07/PubChemPy/blob/master/LICENSE
```

```{image} http://img.shields.io/travis/mcs07/PubChemPy/master.svg?style=flat
:target: https://travis-ci.org/mcs07/PubChemPy
```

```{image} http://img.shields.io/coveralls/mcs07/PubChemPy/master.svg?style=flat
:target: https://coveralls.io/r/mcs07/PubChemPy?branch=master
```

PubChemPy provides a way to interact with PubChem in Python. It allows chemical searches by name, substructure and
similarity, chemical standardization, conversion between chemical file formats, depiction and retrieval of chemical
properties.

```python
>>> from pubchempy import get_compounds, Compound
>>> comp = Compound.from_cid(1423)
>>> print(comp.isomeric_smiles)
CCCCCCCNC1CCCC1CCCCCCC(=O)O
>>> comps = get_compounds('Aspirin', 'name')
>>> print(comps[0].xlogp)
1.2
```

## Installation

Install PubChemPy using:

```
pip install pubchempy
```

Alternatively, try one of the other [installation options].

## Documentation

Full documentation is available at <http://pubchempy.readthedocs.io>.

## Contribute

- Feature ideas and bug reports are welcome on the [Issue Tracker].
- Fork the [source code] on GitHub, make changes and file a pull request.

## License

PubChemPy is licensed under the [MIT license].

[installation options]: http://pubchempy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide/install.html
[issue tracker]: https://github.com/mcs07/PubChemPy/issues
[mit license]: https://github.com/mcs07/PubChemPy/blob/master/LICENSE
[source code]: https://github.com/mcs07/PubChemPy

modules.name: pubchempy
documentation:
PubChemPy

Python interface for the PubChem PUG REST service.
https://github.com/mcs07/PubChemPy
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: pubchempy.request
documentation:
Construct API request from parameters and return the response.

Full specification at http://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pug_rest/PUG_REST.html

functions.name: pubchempy.get
documentation: Request wrapper that automatically handles async requests.

functions.name: pubchempy.get_json
documentation: Request wrapper that automatically parses JSON response and supresses NotFoundError.

functions.name: pubchempy.get_sdf
documentation: Request wrapper that automatically parses SDF response and supresses NotFoundError.

functions.name: pubchempy.get_compounds
documentation:
Retrieve the specified compound records from PubChem.

```
param identifier

The compound identifier to use as a search query.

param namespace

(optional) The identifier type, one of cid, name, smiles, sdf, inchi, inchikey or formula.

param searchtype

(optional) The advanced search type, one of substructure, superstructure or similarity.

param as_dataframe

(optional) Automatically extract the  properties into a pandas
 and return that.
```

functions.name: pubchempy.get_substances
documentation:
Retrieve the specified substance records from PubChem.

```
param identifier

The substance identifier to use as a search query.

param namespace

(optional) The identifier type, one of sid, name or sourceid/<source name>.

param as_dataframe

(optional) Automatically extract the  properties into a pandas
 and return that.
```

functions.name: pubchempy.get_assays
documentation:
Retrieve the specified assay records from PubChem.

```
param identifier

The assay identifier to use as a search query.

param namespace

(optional) The identifier type.
```

functions.name: pubchempy.get_properties
documentation:
Retrieve the specified properties from PubChem.

```
param identifier

The compound, substance or assay identifier to use as a search query.

param namespace

(optional) The identifier type.

param searchtype

(optional) The advanced search type, one of substructure, superstructure or similarity.

param as_dataframe

(optional) Automatically extract the properties into a pandas .
```

functions.name: pubchempy.get_all_sources
documentation: Return a list of all current depositors of substances or assays.

functions.name: pubchempy.download
documentation: Format can be  XML, ASNT/B, JSON, SDF, CSV, PNG, TXT.

functions.name: pubchempy.memoized_property
documentation:
Decorator to create memoized properties.

Used to cache {class}`~pubchempy.Compound` and {class}`~pubchempy.Substance` properties that require an additional
request.

functions.name: pubchempy.deprecated
documentation: Decorator to mark functions as deprecated. A warning will be emitted when the function is used.

functions.name: pubchempy._parse_prop
documentation: Extract property value from record using the given urn search filter.

functions.name: pubchempy.compounds_to_frame
documentation:
Construct a pandas {class}`~pandas.DataFrame` from a list of {class}`~pubchempy.Compound` objects.

Optionally specify a list of the desired {class}`~pubchempy.Compound` properties.

functions.name: pubchempy.substances_to_frame
documentation:
Construct a pandas {class}`~pandas.DataFrame` from a list of {class}`~pubchempy.Substance` objects.

Optionally specify a list of the desired {class}`~pubchempy.Substance` properties.

methods.name: pubchempy.Atom.__init__
documentation:
Initialize with an atom ID, atomic number, coordinates and optional change.

:param int aid: Atom ID
:param int number: Atomic number
:param float x: X coordinate.
:param float y: Y coordinate.
:param float z: (optional) Z coordinate.
:param int charge: (optional) Formal charge on atom.

classes.name: pubchempy.Compound

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.__init__
documentation:
Initialize with a record dict from the PubChem PUG REST service.

For most users, the `from_cid()` class method is probably a better way of creating Compounds.

```
param dict record

A compound record returned by the PubChem PUG REST service.
```

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.record
documentation: The raw compound record returned by the PubChem PUG REST service.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound._setup_atoms
documentation: Derive Atom objects from the record.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound._setup_bonds
documentation: Derive Bond objects from the record.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.from_cid
documentation:
Retrieve the Compound record for the specified CID.

Usage:

```
c = Compound.from_cid(6819)
```

```
param int cid

The PubChem Compound Identifier (CID).
```

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.to_dict
documentation:
Return a dictionary containing Compound data. Optionally specify a list of the desired properties.

synonyms, aids and sids are not included unless explicitly specified using the properties parameter. This is
because they each require an extra request.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.to_series
documentation:
Return a pandas {class}`~pandas.Series` containing Compound data. Optionally specify a list of the desired
properties.

synonyms, aids and sids are not included unless explicitly specified using the properties parameter. This is
because they each require an extra request.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.cid
documentation:
The PubChem Compound Identifier (CID).

:::{note}
When searching using a SMILES or InChI query that is not present in the PubChem Compound database, an
automatically generated record may be returned that contains properties that have been calculated on the
fly. These records will not have a CID property.
:::

documentation: List of element symbols for atoms in this Compound.

documentation: List of {class}`Atoms <pubchempy.Atom>` in this Compound.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.bonds
documentation: List of {class}`Bonds <pubchempy.Bond>` between {class}`Atoms <pubchempy.Atom>` in this Compound.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.synonyms
documentation:
A ranked list of all the names associated with this Compound.

Requires an extra request. Result is cached.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.complexity

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.h_bond_acceptor_count

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.rotatable_bond_count

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.fingerprint
documentation: Raw padded and hex-encoded fingerprint, as returned by the PUG REST API.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.cactvs_fingerprint
documentation:
PubChem CACTVS fingerprint.

Each bit in the fingerprint represents the presence or absence of one of 881 chemical substructures.

More information at ftp://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubchem/specifications/pubchem_fingerprints.txt

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.heavy_atom_count
documentation: Heavy atom count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.isotope_atom_count
documentation: Isotope atom count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.atom_stereo_count
documentation: Atom stereocenter count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.defined_atom_stereo_count
documentation: Defined atom stereocenter count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.undefined_atom_stereo_count
documentation: Undefined atom stereocenter count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.bond_stereo_count
documentation: Bond stereocenter count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.defined_bond_stereo_count
documentation: Defined bond stereocenter count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.undefined_bond_stereo_count
documentation: Undefined bond stereocenter count.

methods.name: pubchempy.Compound.covalent_unit_count
documentation: Covalently-bonded unit count.

Corresponds to a single record from the PubChem Compound database.

The PubChem Compound database is constructed from the Substance database using a standardization and deduplication
process. Each Compound is uniquely identified by a CID.

classes.name: pubchempy.Substance

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.from_sid
documentation:
Retrieve the Substance record for the specified SID.

:param int sid: The PubChem Substance Identifier (SID).

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.to_dict
documentation:
Return a dictionary containing Substance data.

If the properties parameter is not specified, everything except cids and aids is included. This is because the
aids and cids properties each require an extra request to retrieve.

```
param properties

(optional) A list of the desired properties.
```

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.to_series
documentation:
Return a pandas {class}`~pandas.Series` containing Substance data.

If the properties parameter is not specified, everything except cids and aids is included. This is because the
aids and cids properties each require an extra request to retrieve.

```
param properties

(optional) A list of the desired properties.
```

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.sid
documentation: The PubChem Substance Idenfitier (SID).

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.synonyms
documentation: A ranked list of all the names associated with this Substance.

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.source_name
documentation: The name of the PubChem depositor that was the source of this Substance.

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.source_id
documentation: Unique ID for this Substance within those from the same PubChem depositor source.

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.standardized_cid
documentation:
The CID of the Compound that was produced when this Substance was standardized.

May not exist if this Substance was not standardizable.

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.standardized_compound
documentation:
Return the {class}`~pubchempy.Compound` that was produced when this Substance was standardized.

Requires an extra request. Result is cached.

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.deposited_compound
documentation:
Return a {class}`~pubchempy.Compound` produced from the unstandardized Substance record as deposited.

The resulting {class}`~pubchempy.Compound` will not have a `cid` and will be missing most properties.

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.cids
documentation:
A list of all CIDs for Compounds that were produced when this Substance was standardized.

Requires an extra request. Result is cached.

methods.name: pubchempy.Substance.aids
documentation:
A list of all AIDs for Assays associated with this Substance.

Requires an extra request. Result is cached.

Corresponds to a single record from the PubChem Substance database.

The PubChem Substance database contains all chemical records deposited in PubChem in their most raw form, before
any significant processing is applied. As a result, it contains duplicates, mixtures, and some records that don't
make chemical sense. This means that Substance records contain fewer calculated properties, however they do have
additional information about the original source that deposited the record.

The PubChem Compound database is constructed from the Substance database using a standardization and deduplication
process. Hence each Compound may be derived from a number of different Substances.

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.from_aid
documentation:
Retrieve the Assay record for the specified AID.

:param int aid: The PubChem Assay Identifier (AID).

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.to_dict
documentation:
Return a dictionary containing Assay data.

If the properties parameter is not specified, everything is included.

```
param properties

(optional) A list of the desired properties.
```

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.aid
documentation: The PubChem Substance Idenfitier (SID).

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.name
documentation: The short assay name, used for display purposes.

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.description
documentation: Description

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.project_category
documentation:
A category to distinguish projects funded through MLSCN, MLPCN or from literature.

Possible values include mlscn, mlpcn, mlscn-ap, mlpcn-ap, literature-extracted, literature-author,
literature-publisher, rnaigi.

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.comments
documentation: Comments and additional information.

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.results
documentation: A list of dictionaries containing details of the results from this Assay.

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.target
documentation: A list of dictionaries containing details of the Assay targets.

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.revision
documentation: Revision identifier for textual description.

methods.name: pubchempy.Assay.aid_version
documentation: Incremented when the original depositor updates the record.

classes.name: pubchempy.PubChemPyDeprecationWarning
documentation: Warning category for deprecated features.
def add_columns_to_frame(dataframe, id_col, id_namespace, add_cols):
# Existing dataframe with some identifier column
# But consider what to do if the identifier column is an index?
# What about having the Compound/Substance object as a column?

classes.name: pubchempy.PubChemPyError
documentation: Base class for all PubChemPy exceptions.

classes.name: pubchempy.ResponseParseError
documentation: PubChem response is uninterpretable.

classes.name: pubchempy.PubChemHTTPError
documentation: Generic error class to handle all HTTP error codes.

classes.name: pubchempy.BadRequestError
documentation: Request is improperly formed (syntax error in the URL, POST body, etc.).

classes.name: pubchempy.NotFoundError
documentation: The input record was not found (e.g. invalid CID).

classes.name: pubchempy.MethodNotAllowedError
documentation: Request not allowed (such as invalid MIME type in the HTTP Accept header).

classes.name: pubchempy.TimeoutError
documentation:
The request timed out, from server overload or too broad a request.

See {ref}`Avoiding TimeoutError <avoiding_timeouterror>` for more information.

classes.name: pubchempy.UnimplementedError
documentation: The requested operation has not (yet) been implemented by the server.

classes.name: pubchempy.ServerError
documentation: Some problem on the server side (such as a database server down, etc.).
